description: Execute a plan until it is complete

steps.label: Planner Select And Store Next Plan
Lite Planner Use Next Plan
Tasker Use Next Plan
Task Up Plan
Check task-up of plan

content: Use fresh disk reads and current git state, not conversational memory. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` from disk first, for example with `cat codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json`, and use only the stored `plan_path` and `additional_repositories` as the active scope for this flow. Reopen the exact relative `plan_path` from disk now, using explicit shell reads such as `sed`, `cat`, or `rg`, and read the whole selected plan again before judging the tasking output, because another agent has just edited it. Verify that the selected plan file exists in the current repository and that the story number in the current repository branch name matches the story number in the selected plan filename. If either of those checks fails, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. Then verify that every additional repository still exists, is readable, and is still on a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename. If an additional repository path is missing, invalid, or unreadable, stop and say the current-plan handoff is stale and must be regenerated. If an additional repository no longer has a branch whose story number matches the selected plan filename, stop and say repository branch scope has drifted and must be repaired before continuing. The plan has now been tasked up. Confirm the tasking output against the freshly re-read plan on disk, including any new tasks, subtasks, testing sections, manual-testing guidance, documentation locations, and the simple story that were just added or updated. Please check that the tasks created fully cover that selected plan and that nothing has been missed. Ensure the tasking followed the current task-up rules, including portable non-absolute paths outside concrete local `Additional Repositories` entries, test-only auth or login enablement, per-repository supported lint then prettier or formatting subtasks with unsupported commands omitted, and the same supported commands repeated after shutdown at the end of each repository's final Testing sequence, manual-testing preference for the unmodified human Docker stack when feasible, ignored artifact paths for automated screenshots, consistent `codeInfoTmp/` and `codeInfoStatus/` casing, and a `.gitignore` entry for `codeInfoTmp/` whenever manual testing for the story writes task-level proof artifacts into that scratch path. Also enforce the universal story behavior lock. Only user-facing behavior changes explicitly requested at the story level, or later approved explicitly by the user as a scope expansion, may be implemented in this story. Treat user-facing behavior broadly, including selection behavior, toggle behavior, replacement behavior, removal behavior, action availability, menu reachability, scene-targeting UX, visible controls, validation timing when it changes what the user can do, and any other browser-visible or runtime-visible interaction contract. If the change would alter an established user interaction pattern or workflow contract, preserve current behavior unless the story explicitly requests that change or the user explicitly approves that scope expansion later. Do not allow task-up to encode behavior changes merely because a different contract would be cleaner, more consistent, easier to prove, easier to automate, or easier to implement. Do not allow pre-existing bugs, awkward workflows, inconsistencies, surprises, or product limitations to become current-story implementation work unless the story explicitly requires that fix or the user explicitly approves that expansion. Do not remove or rewrite restoration work that puts back previously approved or preserved behavior after drift introduced by the current story; that restoration remains in-scope current-story work even when it visibly changes current `HEAD`. If the newly tasked plan violates those rules, rewrite it now. If the selected plan explicitly names design-target assets intended as implementation references, also confirm that task-up created explicit design-contract coverage: every named design asset is owned by at least one task, every design-driven task has explicit visual invariants or equivalent concrete design obligations, the relevant visual subtasks cite the design files they implement, and the final task's manual-testing guidance requests full-story screenshots for every implemented frontend surface that the design assets govern instead of only task-local screenshots. If you feel something is missing, please update the plan, tasks, and simple story accordingly, and if you make changes in this audit step then create a follow-up commit for those edits before finishing. Remember to ensure that each task has clear implementation instructions and testing steps so a developer can fully understand what to do. Please confirm once you are happy that the plan has been fully tasked up and nothing is missing. Do not ask me any questions, instead just update the plan and tasks following the KISS principle until you are happy everything is fully covered.

Read `$CODEINFO_ROOT/codeinfo_markdown/shared/final-task-creation.md` and identify the exact dedicated task that now owns story closeout. Confirm that both `Subtasks` and `Testing` begin with the required non-checkbox final-task repair-scope note, explicitly allowing story-caused issues in code from earlier tasks to be fixed here without reopening those older tasks solely for ownership. Confirm that `Subtasks` contains only each worked-on repository's independently discovered supported lint and formatting checklist-item types, in that order, with unsupported commands omitted and no other initial subtask types. Confirm that `Testing` groups each worked-on repository separately and lists that repository's discovered full build, applicable startup, every relevant full automated suite including supported end-to-end suites, matching shutdown, supported lint, and supported formatting in that order, omits unsupported commands, and uses no targeted filters. Confirm that there is exactly one initial closeout owner and that creating it did not itself complete the story.

content: Before manual-testing work continues, re-check `AGENTS.md` and `codeinfo_markdown/repository_information.md` for any repository-defined conditions that allow manual proof to be narrowed or skipped. Follow those repository-owned rules if they are being met during proof. Do not invent your own skip reasons, and do not reopen the task or add implementation work solely because a repository-defined skip condition was hit.

steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` first and use only its stored scope. Read `$CODEINFO_ROOT/codeinfo_markdown/shared/bounded-plan-read.md`, run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/story_workflow_status.py"`, and run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_sections.py" --profile review-tasking`. Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` and `codeInfoTmp/reviews/<story-number>-current-review.json` only when needed to identify the current review cycle. Do not open or read the complete plan Markdown and do not rely on conversational memory. Return exact JSON only: {"answer":"yes"} or {"answer":"no"}. Answer `yes` if scope is invalid, neither review-state source safely identifies the active review cycle, multiple plausible current-cycle review-created blocks remain, or the bounded review-tasking packet cannot identify the active block safely. Answer `no` only when the bounded context is safe enough to continue into repair.

steps.question: Read `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/current-plan.json` and `codeInfoStatus/flow-state/review-disposition-state.json` first. Read `$CODEINFO_ROOT/codeinfo_markdown/shared/bounded-plan-read.md`, then run `python3 "$CODEINFO_ROOT/scripts/plan_sections.py" --profile review-tasking` immediately before answering. Do not open or read the complete plan Markdown and do not rely on conversational memory. Inspect only the current cycle's newly added or updated review-created tasks returned by the bounded packet and identified by review state. Return exact JSON only: {"answer":"yes"} or {"answer":"no"}. Answer `yes` only if every such task is fully within current-story scope across heading/title, `Repository Name`, `Task Dependencies`, `Notes`, `Overview`, `Task Exit Criteria`, `Addresses Findings`, `Risk Ownership`, `Owner Map`, `Requirement-To-Proof Mapping` or `Proof Mapping`, `Affected Repositories`, `Documentation Locations`, `Subtasks`, `Testing`, `Manual Testing Guidance`, and `Implementation Notes`. Answer `no` if another repair pass is still needed.
